properties.Test Case: An overflow condition for a Device 16x9 book (only) will show scroll bars instead of the RED text. There will be no danger triangle on the thumbnail. Try various page layouts and vertical alignments and multiple paragraphs. Try adding new paragraphs at the end. N.B. Current limitation: Device layout, vertically centered, overflowing multiple paragraphs has problems outlined in BL-12750 which has not been fixed. Also note BL-14260 is an issue we will live with.
properties.Steps: - An overflow condition for a Device 16x9 book (only) will show scroll bars instead of the RED text. There will be no danger triangle on the thumbnail. Try various page layouts and vertical alignments and multiple paragraphs. Try adding new paragraphs at the end. N.B. Current limitation: Device layout, vertically centered, overflowing multiple paragraphs has problems outlined in BL-12750 which has not been fixed. Also note BL-14260 is an issue we will live with.

properties.Test Case: Copy/Paste scripture text from a Paratext project into a Bloom book to ensure that a) the text is not duplicated; b) superscripts for verse numbers are handled reasonably. Bloom has code to find "\v N" and convert it to superscript, but this doesn't work when the incoming text is in html because of the markup. In my 6.1-6.3 testing w/PTX 9.5, pasting with Ctrl-V pasted as html and therefore did not give superscripts. Ctrl-Shift-V pasted as text and did. If superscripts are added, those should be reversible using the standard ckeditor control. Originally this said: superscripts for verse numbers are formatted as such when copying from "Unformatted" view; otherwise, from "Preview" & "Standard" you get unformatted numbers for verses, and "Formatted" gives backslash codes. Note, "HTML clipboard" prefix is ok.
properties.Steps:
Copy/Paste scripture text from a Paratext project into a Bloom book to ensure that a) the text is not duplicated; b) superscripts for verse numbers are handled reasonably.
Bloom has code to find "\v N" and convert it to superscript, but this doesn't work when the incoming text is in html because of the markup. In my 6.1-6.3 testing w/PTX 9.5, pasting with Ctrl-V pasted as html and therefore did not give superscripts. Ctrl-Shift-V pasted as text and did.
If superscripts are added, those should be reversible using the standard ckeditor control.

Originally this said: superscripts for verse numbers are formatted as such when copying from "Unformatted" view; otherwise, from "Preview" & "Standard" you get unformatted numbers for verses, and "Formatted" gives backslash codes. Note, "HTML clipboard" prefix is ok.

properties.Test Case: Open a Bloom shell book and a Word document. Copy text, then REPLACE text in Bloom book using CTRL-A & PASTE through all pages of the book and monitor performance.
properties.Steps: Open a Bloom shell book and a Word document. Copy text, then REPLACE text in Bloom book using CTRL-A & PASTE through all pages of the book and monitor performance.

properties.Test Case: Select a word, give it bold, italics, etc. Give it a color as well (not via the style, but rather via the pop up character formatter that you use for bold). Copy the text to a new box. Verify that it all appears correctly.
properties.Steps: Select a word, give it bold, italics, etc. Give it a color as well (not via the style, but rather via the pop up character formatter that you use for bold). Copy the text to a new box. Verify that it all appears correctly.
